prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response: A bold request! Based on general trends and common misconceptions, I'm going to take a guess: you probably think that humans only use 10% of their brains. This is a widespread myth that has been debunked by experts in neuroscience and psychology. In reality, brain imaging techniques have shown that different parts of the brain are active at different times, and even the supposedly "idle" areas are still performing important background functions, like regulating the body's automatic systems. So, it's likely that you, like many people, have been misinformed about the extent to which we utilize our brainpower. Am I right?
